mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or returns following declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals a deteriorating labor market that leads investors to revise down earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the effect disappears (t-test p > 0.05) across a 5-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating retail sales growth occurring while the federal funds rate remains at an elevated level jointly signals that monetary tightening is starting to destroy consumer demand, a combination that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month more strongly than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth turns negative while FEDFUNDS is above its trailing 12-month median
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with slowing consumption
failure_condition: The joint-trigger signal underperforms the single-series RSAFS-only signal in out-of-sample Rank IC, or negative returns fail to materialize in more than 50% of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerating during a late-cycle expansion signals continued capacity utilization gains and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; the same acceleration in an early-cycle or recessionary regime does not carry the same predictive power.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) specifically within identified late-cycle windows, or the effect is indistinguishable from early-cycle windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in housing starts reflects strengthening forward-looking consumer demand and construction-linked economic activity, which propagates quickly into equity risk sentiment and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive relationship (Rank IC < 0.02) with HOUST 3-month trend over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth and an increase in the unemployment rate jointly indicate an emerging labor market downturn that markets underprice individually, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month; neither series alone reliably predicts the same magnitude of decline.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change turns negative while UNRATE rises simultaneously
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal produces no incremental predictive power (Rank IC gain < 0.02) over the UNRATE-only threshold signal in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above a restrictive threshold, tightening financial conditions compress equity valuations sharply within a short window as markets reprice discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 5.0% for the first time in the cycle
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following the threshold crossing are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the crossing event shows positive returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained slowdown in CPI inflation combined with continued industrial production growth during a post-inflation-shock recovery regime signals disinflation without demand destruction, supporting margin recovery and positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth declines for 3 consecutive months while INDPRO growth remains positive
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during identified disinflation-with-growth episodes, or the effect reverses sign outside the post-shock regime window.
